definition: Lasting for a very short time; transient.
usage_notes: Often used to describe beauty, trends, moments, or digital content
Carries a slightly poetic or reflective tone
Also used technically in computing (ephemeral storage, ephemeral ports)

etymology.origin_summary: From Greek 'ephemeros' — lasting only a day. 'epi-' (on) + 'hemera' (day).

examples: Social media trends are ephemeral — here today, forgotten tomorrow.
The ephemeral beauty of cherry blossoms makes them all the more treasured.

confusions.difference: 'Temporary' implies planned duration; 'ephemeral' implies something naturally short-lived.
Nearly synonymous, but 'ephemeral' is more formal and literary.
